bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same utility structure, so valueF gives 1.0 utility on every issue. My top bid gives utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. Starting at the best possible position is standard early-game strategy with no time pressure yet.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), so there's no need to concede much. I'll stay close to my top bid but make a small concession on issueA (my least important issue, weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate, while holding firm on issueD (most important, weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3).

The opponent is firmly holding at their maximum utility bid (all valueA) and hasn't conceded at all. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll concede slightly on issueA (my least important issue at 0.1 weight) by dropping to valueD, and keep my high-value choices on issueD (most important) and issueC. This keeps my utility high while signaling willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is stubbornly holding near their best values (all A's), with only a tiny concession on issueC (valueB in their last offer). It's very early (t=0.06), so I don't need to concede much. My highest-weight issue is issueD (0.4), so I'll hold firm there. I can afford to concede slightly on issueA (lowest weight 0.1) and issueB (0.2) while keeping issueC and issueD at valueF. I'll try valueC on issueA and valueE on issueB to signal some movement without giving up much utility.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueC (valueA → valueB → valueC) and issueD (valueA → valueA → valueB), while staying firm on issueA and issueB at valueA. My highest-weight issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I should hold firm there. Since issueA has the lowest weight (0.1), I can concede it fully to valueA. I'll also slightly concede on issueB while keeping issueC and issueD at valueF to protect my most important issues. At t=0.08 we're still early, so I can afford to stay near my top utility.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueB (valueA → valueC) and issueC (valueA → valueC), but still holding firm on issueD (valueA) which is my most important issue. I need to hold firm on issueD (valueF) since it has 0.4 weight, and I can afford to concede more on issueA (0.1 weight) and issueB (0.2 weight). At t=0.1, we're still early, so I should maintain high utility while showing some flexibility. My last bid was {A:valueA, B:valueD, C:valueF, D:valueF} with utility ≈ 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.02 + 0.136 + 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.856. I'll try conceding slightly on issueC while keeping issueD at valueF, and adjust issueB upward slightly to avoid repeating.

The opponent's latest offer gives me utility: issueA(valueB)=0.35×0.1 + issueB(valueA)=0.20×0.2 + issueC(valueC)=0.52×0.3 + issueD(valueE)=0.84×0.4 = 0.035+0.04+0.156+0.336 = 0.567. The opponent seems to be slowly conceding on issueC and issueD (issueD jumped to valueE in their last offer, which is great for me). I should hold firm on issueD (my highest weight) and issueC, while being more flexible on issueA and issueB. At t=0.12 we're still early, so I can target ~0.85+ utility. I'll keep issueD at valueF, issueC at valueE, and concede a bit more on issueB to valueB, keeping issueA at valueA.

The opponent is slowly conceding - their latest offer gives me utility: issueA=valueC(0.52*0.1=0.052), issueB=valueA(0.20*0.2=0.04), issueC=valueC(0.52*0.3=0.156), issueD=valueD(0.68*0.4=0.272) = ~0.52. We're still early (t=0.14), so I should stay firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueC) while making small concessions on low-weight ones. The opponent seems to care about issueC (moving from valueA to valueC) and issueD (now at valueD). I'll hold issueD at valueF and issueC at valueE, concede slightly on issueB, and keep issueA low since it's least important to me.
